questions.text: A rock is dropped from the top of a tall tower. Half a second later another rock, twice as massive as the first, is dropped. Ignoring air resistance.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The distance between the rocks increases while both are falling.
options.explanation: The distance between the rocks increases while both are falling: This is true. While both rocks are accelerating downwards due to gravity at the same rate (approximately 9.8 m/s²), the first rock has a head start of half a second. This means it has already covered some distance before the second rock is even released. Since both rocks continue to accelerate at the same rate, the distance between them will continue to increase as they fall. The first rock will always be ahead because it was released first.

options.text: The acceleration is greater for the more massive rock.
options.explanation: False: The acceleration due to gravity is constant (9.8 m/s²) for all objects, regardless of mass, when air resistance is ignored.

options.text: They strike the ground more than half a second apart.
options.explanation: False: Since both rocks experience the same gravitational acceleration, the time gap between their impacts equals the time gap of their releases, which is half a second.

options.text: They strike the ground with the same kinetic energy.
options.explanation: False: Kinetic energy depends on both mass and velocity. Although both rocks reach the same velocity upon impact, the second rock, having twice the mass, will have twice the kinetic energy.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: The distance between the rocks increases while both are falling. The first rock is released before the second and starts accelerating immediately due to gravity. When the second rock is released, it begins to fall with the same acceleration but starts from rest, whereas the first rock already has velocity. Consequently, the distance between them increases. Option B is incorrect because all objects accelerate at the same rate in free fall regardless of mass. Option C is incorrect because the time difference between the releases is maintained until impact. Option D is incorrect because the second rock, being more massive, will strike the ground with greater kinetic energy.

questions.text: A spring-loaded gun can fire a projectile to a height h if it is fired straight up. If the same gun is pointed at an angle of 45° from the vertical, what maximum height can now be reached by the projectile?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option assumes an incorrect calculation of the vertical component of the velocity at a 45° launch angle. The height is not reduced to a quarter; the vertical component needs to be considered.
This answer miscalculates the effect of the angle on the vertical component of velocity. The reduction factor is not correct.

Using energy conservation in the first situation presented K=U gives the initial velocity as v = √2gh . The gun will fire at this velocity regardless of the angle. In the second scenario, the ball starts with the same initial energy but at the top will have both KE and PE so will be at a lower height. The velocity at the top will be equal to the vx at the beginning.
This option misrepresents the effect of the angle on the vertical component. The factor indicates a misunderstanding of the correct trigonometric application.

questions.explanation: When the projectile is fired straight up, all of its initial velocity contributes to reaching the height h. At an angle of 45°, only the vertical component of the velocity (v_y = v * sin(45°)) contributes to the maximum height. Since sin(45°) = 1/√2, the vertical component becomes v/√2. Using the formula for maximum height, (v_y^2)/2g, and substituting v_y with v/√2, the height becomes (v^2/2g) * (1/2) = h/2. Thus, the correct answer is h/2. Other options incorrectly modify the vertical component or misapply the trigonometric relationships.

questions.text: An object is projected vertically upward from ground level. It rises to a maximum height H. If air resistance is negligible, which of the following must be true for the object when it is at a height H/2?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Its speed is half of its initial speed.
options.explanation: This is incorrect because speed does not linearly decrease by half at half the maximum height. The relationship between speed and height is not linear due to the conservation of energy.

options.text: Its kinetic energy is half of its initial kinetic energy.
options.explanation: Energy is conserved so the term mgh + ½ mv2 must remain constant. As the object rises it loses K and gains U. Since the height is H/2 it has gained half of the total potential energy it will end up with which means it must have lost half of its kinetic energy, so its K is half of what it was when it was first shot.

options.text: Its potential energy is half of its initial potential energy.
options.explanation: This is incorrect because the initial potential energy at ground level is zero. At height H/2, the potential energy is half of the maximum potential energy it would have at height H, not half of its initial potential energy.

options.text: Its total mechanical energy is half of its initial value.
options.explanation: This is incorrect because total mechanical energy is conserved in the absence of air resistance, so it remains constant throughout the motion.

questions.explanation: In the absence of air resistance, the total mechanical energy of the object is conserved, which means it remains constant throughout the object's motion. The total mechanical energy is the sum of kinetic energy (K) and potential energy (U). Initially, at ground level, the object has maximum kinetic energy and zero potential energy. As it rises, it loses kinetic energy and gains potential energy.When the object reaches a height of H/2, it has gained half of the maximum potential energy it will achieve at height H. Consequently, it must have lost half of its initial kinetic energy to maintain the conservation of total mechanical energy. Therefore, the correct statement is that its kinetic energy is half of its initial kinetic energy.Option A is incorrect because speed is not directly halved at half the maximum height. Option C is incorrect because the initial potential energy is zero, and potential energy at any height is measured from that initial state. Option D is incorrect because the total mechanical energy remains constant, not halved.

questions.text: Which of the following is commutative?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Vector addition is commutative because for any two vectors A and B, the sum A + B is equal to B + A. This property is a characteristic of commutative operations.

Vector multiplication, specifically the cross product, is not commutative. For vectors A and B, A × B is not equal to B × A; in fact, A × B = –(B × A). Thus, vector multiplication does not satisfy the commutative property.

Vector subtraction is not commutative because for two vectors A and B, A - B is not equal to B - A. The result of subtraction depends on the order of the operands, which violates the commutative property.

options.text: Both A and B are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Only vector addition is commutative, while vector multiplication is not. Thus, both A and B cannot be correct.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: Vector addition. Vector addition is commutative because the order in which two vectors are added does not affect the result. For two vectors A and B, A + B = B + A.Option B, vector multiplication, is incorrect because the cross product of two vectors is not commutative; A × B does not equal B × A.Option C, vector subtraction, is incorrect because subtraction is inherently non-commutative. The order matters, thus A - B ≠ B - A.Option D is incorrect because it suggests both A and B are commutative, which is not true. Only vector addition is commutative.

questions.text: A baseball of mass 0.25 kg is thrown in the air with an initial speed of 30m/s but because of air resistance, the ball returns to the ground with a speed of 27m/s. Find the work done by air resistance.

Here, u = 10 m/s, a = -9.8 m/s², and t = 2 seconds. Substituting these values, v = 10 + (-9.8) * 2 = 9.6 m/s. Option A is correct because it matches this calculation. questions.text: A projectile is fired from ground level with an initial velocity of 50m/s and an initial angle of elevation of 37° as shown below . Assuming g=10m/s². Find the total horizontal distance traveled. (sin37°=0.6 ;cos37°=0.8)
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect. This option underestimates the range by not considering the correct horizontal velocity component and time of flight.
Option B is incorrect. This is an intermediate calculation mistake, possibly from misapplying the time of flight or horizontal velocity.
Option C is correct. This takes into account the correct horizontal component of velocity and the total time the projectile is in the air.
Option D is incorrect. The problem provides enough information to calculate the range using projectile motion equations.

questions.explanation: To solve for the total horizontal distance traveled by the projectile, we first calculate the horizontal and vertical components of the initial velocity. The horizontal component (Vx) is given by V * cos(37°) = 50 m/s * 0.8 = 40 m/s. The vertical component (Vy) is given by V * sin(37°) = 50 m/s * 0.6 = 30 m/s.Next, we find the time of flight by using the vertical motion equation. The time to reach maximum height is Vy / g = 30 m/s / 10 m/s² = 3 seconds. Since the ascent and descent times are equal, the total time of flight is 6 seconds.Finally, the horizontal distance (range) is calculated using the equation range = Vx * total time = 40 m/s * 6 s = 240 meters.Option C is correct because it correctly uses the horizontal velocity and total time of flight. Option A is incorrect because it underestimates these values, Option B does not correctly apply these calculations, and Option D is incorrect because sufficient information is given.

questions.text: A person walks 30m east and then 40m north. What is the difference between the travelled distance and the displacement?

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect. The displacement is not zero because the person did change their position.

Option C is incorrect. 50m is the displacement, not the difference between the traveled distance and displacement.
Option D is incorrect. 70m is the total distance traveled, not the difference between distance and displacement.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: 20m. The person travels a total distance of 30m east and 40m north, which sums up to 70m. The displacement, being the straight-line distance between the starting and ending points, forms a right triangle with the legs of 30m and 40m. Using the Pythagorean theorem, the displacement is calculated as √(30² + 40²) = 50m. Therefore, the difference between the traveled distance and the displacement is 70m - 50m = 20m. Option A is incorrect because the displacement is not zero. Option C is incorrect because 50m is the displacement, not the difference. Option D is incorrect because 70m is the total distance traveled.

questions.text: A car is traveling at 40km/hr and the driver puts on the brakes bringing the car to rest in a time of 6s. What is the magnitude of the average acceleration of the car?

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C: 24,000 km/hr². To find the average acceleration, use the formula a = (v_f - v_i) / t, where v_f is the final velocity (0 km/hr), v_i is the initial velocity (40 km/hr), and t is the time (6 seconds). Converting the units to km/hr² by multiplying by 3600 to account for time in seconds results in an acceleration of 24,000 km/hr². questions.text: A 10 kg wagon rests on an inclined plane. The plane makes an angle of 30° with the horizontal. Approximately how large is the force required to keep the wagon from sliding down the plane( Note: sin30°=0.5;cos30°=0.866)

Option C is incorrect. This value is higher than necessary to balance the gravitational force.
Option D is incorrect. This represents the entire weight of the wagon, not just the component along the plane.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: 49N. To find the force required to keep the wagon from sliding down, we calculate the component of the gravitational force acting parallel to the inclined plane. This force is given by the formula: F_parallel = m * g * sin(θ), where m is the mass of the wagon (10 kg), g is the acceleration due to gravity (approximately 9.8 m/s²), and θ is the angle of the incline (30°). Substituting these values, we have F_parallel = 10 kg * 9.8 m/s² * 0.5 = 49N. This is the force required to prevent the wagon from sliding down. The other options either underestimate or overestimate the force needed.

questions.text: Centrifugal force is an apparent outward force during circular motion. It has been described as a reaction force according to Newton's third law. Which of the following statements is most likely to be correct regarding centrifugal force?

Centrifugal force exists only when tension or a normal force provides centripetal acceleration
Centrifugal force always acts antiparallel to the centripetal force vector
Centrifugal force is result of repulsive electrostatic interactions.

questions.explanation: The statement that centrifugal force always acts antiparallel to the centripetal force vector is likely to be correct because they are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction according to Newton's third law of motion. While the centripetal force is directed towards the center of the circular motion, the centrifugal force is an apparent outward force that emerges as a result of an object's inertia trying to continue in a straight line. Therefore, to maintain circular motion, the two forces act in opposite directions, with the centripetal force pulling the object towards the center and the centrifugal force pushing it away from the center.

questions.text: In angular motion, Newton's second law of motion is:

questions.explanation: The correct answer is τ = Iα, which is the angular analog of Newton's second law. This is similar to the linear form, F = ma, where force (F) is the product of mass (m) and linear acceleration (a).Option A, F = ma, is incorrect as it applies to linear motion, not angular motion. questions.text: A bullet is fired from a short gun to shoot a bag of sand. It hits the bag and goes through it.In the given situation, when the builet hits the bag of sand, the total momentum of the bullet

Gravity only affects the vertical motion, not the horizontal. Horizontal acceleration is not a factor in the time it takes to reach the ground.

questions.explanation: The time it takes for an object to reach the ground is determined by its initial vertical velocity and the height from which it is released, not by its horizontal velocity. Since both balls are released simultaneously with zero vertical velocity, they are only affected by gravity, which acts vertically. Thus, they reach the ground at the same time. The horizontal velocity does not influence the time it takes for the balls to fall. Option A is incorrect because the size of the balls does not affect their fall time in the absence of air resistance. Option C is incorrect because while the same vertical height ensures the same fall time, the question focuses on the effect of horizontal velocity. Option D is incorrect because horizontal acceleration does not affect vertical fall time.

questions.text: Missiles I, II, III, and IV are launched at 35o, 45o, 55o and 65o, respectively from the origin. The shortest distance will be covered by
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Launched at 35°, which is less than the optimal angle for maximum range, but the difference between this angle and the optimal angle is not the biggest.
Launched at 45°, which is the optimal angle for maximum range in ideal conditions.
Launched at 55°, which is greater than the optimal angle for maximum range, but the difference between this angle and the optimal angle is not the biggest.
Launched at 65°, which is farthest from the optimal angle for maximum range, resulting in the shortest distance.

questions.explanation: In projectile motion, the optimal angle for maximum range is 45°. Missiles launched at angles deviating from this will cover shorter distances. Missile IV, launched at 65°, is furthest from the optimal angle, resulting in the shortest range. Missile II, launched at 45°, achieves maximum range, while Missiles I and III, launched at 35° and 55°, respectively, cover distances shorter than Missile II but longer than Missile IV.

questions.text: A two dimensional motion under the acceleration due to gravity is known as
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Linear motion refers to movement along a straight line, which is typically one-dimensional. It does not involve both horizontal and vertical components under gravity.
Circular motion involves an object moving along a circular path, which is influenced by centripetal force rather than gravitational acceleration in two dimensions.
Vibratory motion refers to oscillations about an equilibrium position, such as a pendulum, and does not describe two-dimensional motion under gravity.
Projectile motion describes the two-dimensional motion of an object under the influence of gravity, where it has both horizontal and vertical components.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is projectile motion. Projectile motion occurs when an object is thrown or projected into the air and moves along a curved path under the influence of gravity. It involves both horizontal and vertical motion components, making it two-dimensional. Linear motion is incorrect as it refers to one-dimensional movement. Circular motion is incorrect as it involves movement along a circular path. Vibratory motion is incorrect as it involves oscillations, not two-dimensional motion under gravity.

questions.text: Which of the following laws of conservation is used in the propulsion of rockets?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The conservation of heat is not directly related to the propulsion mechanism of rockets.
While mass plays a role in rocket propulsion, it's specifically the conservation of momentum that is the primary principle.
Although energy conservation is important in the broader context, it is not the key principle in rocket propulsion.
The conservation of momentum is the correct principle, as it describes how the momentum lost by the expulsion of gas is gained by the rocket, propelling it forward.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Momentum. Rocket propulsion is explained by Newton's third law of motion: for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. When a rocket expels gas downwards, the momentum lost by the gas is gained by the rocket, propelling it upwards. This is a direct application of the conservation of momentum. The other options are incorrect because:Heat: While heat is generated and plays a role in energy transfer, it does not directly govern the propulsion mechanism of rockets.Mass: Mass is a component of the momentum equation, but the conservation principle used is momentum, not mass.Energy: Energy conservation is relevant in rocket science, but it is not the principle specifically used to explain propulsion.

questions.text: The type of motion represented by the given velocity-time graph is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Variable motion occurs when an object's velocity changes irregularly over time.
Instantaneous motion is characterized by velocity at a specific moment in time, but it is not a type of motion represented over a time period in a graph.
Uniformly accelerated motion is when an object's velocity changes at a constant rate, represented by a straight line on a velocity-time graph.
Non-uniformly accelerated motion occurs when an object's velocity changes at a variable rate, depicted by a curved line on a velocity-time graph.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is uniformly accelerated motion. This type of motion is shown on a velocity-time graph as a straight line, indicating constant acceleration. Option A, variable motion, refers to inconsistent changes in velocity over time. Option B, instantaneous motion, is not applicable as it refers to velocity at a specific instant, not over a duration. Option D, non-uniformly accelerated motion, would result in a curved line on the graph due to the changing rate of acceleration.

questions.text: A freely falling ball bounces back when it hits the ground. At the point where the ball hits the ground, the impulse on the ball as compared to the momentum of the ball will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The impulse cannot be less than the change in momentum because impulse is defined as the change in momentum.
If the impulse were zero, there would be no change in momentum, which contradicts the fact that the ball bounces back.
Impulse is equal to the change in momentum of the ball when it hits the ground and bounces back.
Impulse cannot be greater than the change in momentum as they are equivalent by definition, given no external forces.

questions.explanation: The impulse imparted on an object during a collision is equal to the change in momentum of that object. In this scenario, as the ball hits the ground and bounces back, its momentum changes direction, implying a change in momentum. Therefore, the impulse is equal to this change in momentum, making Option C the correct answer. Option A is incorrect because impulse, by definition, equals the change in momentum, not less. Option B is incorrect because a non-zero impulse is required to change the direction of the ball's momentum. Option D is incorrect as impulse cannot be more than the change in momentum; they are equivalent.

questions.text: A packet of mass (m) is unloaded from a truck by using inclined plane from a height (h) asshown in the given figure.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: A: The packet moves without any external force.
options.explanation: This option assumes no external forces are acting, which is incorrect as gravity acts on the packet.

options.text: B: The packet accelerates due to gravitational force.
options.explanation: This option partially describes the scenario but lacks specificity about the inclined plane's effect.

options.text: C: The packet remains stationary unless pushed.
options.explanation: This option ignores the effect of gravity on the inclined plane, which causes the packet to move.

options.text: D: The gravitational component along the plane causes the packet to slide down.
options.explanation: This is the correct answer as it accurately describes the role of gravity and the inclined plane in moving the packet.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: The gravitational component along the plane causes the packet to slide down. When a packet is placed on an inclined plane, gravity acts on it, causing it to accelerate down the plane. The force of gravity can be divided into two components: one perpendicular to the plane and one parallel to it. The parallel component causes the packet to slide down the plane. Options A, B, and C are incorrect because they either ignore the effects of gravity or fail to account for the inclined plane's role in the motion.

questions.text: An elevator lifts a weight of 400 N through a height of 10 m in 20 s. The power of the motor is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Power is calculated as Work Done divided by Time, where Work Done = Force x Distance.
This option incorrectly calculates the power. Double-check the formula: Power = Work Done / Time.
This option assumes an incorrect calculation of Work Done or misapplies the formula for Power.
This option is significantly higher than the correct power calculation. Ensure you are using the correct values in the formula.

questions.explanation: To calculate the power of the motor, you need to find the work done and then divide it by the time. Work Done is calculated as Force multiplied by Distance. Here, Work Done = 400 N * 10 m = 4000 J (Joules). Power is then Work Done divided by Time: Power = 4000 J / 20 s = 200 W. Therefore, the correct answer is 200 W. Options B, C, and D result from incorrect applications of the power formula, either miscalculating Work Done or incorrectly dividing by Time.

questions.text: The given figure shows the first attempt of a long jump of an athlete in Olympics.Which of the following factors will MAINLY help him to break his own record in the next attempt?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Angle of projection of the athlete
options.explanation: A) Angle of projection of the athlete: In projectile motion (like a long jump or shot put, as depicted in the image), the angle at which an object is launched significantly affects its range (horizontal distance). For maximum range, an optimal angle of projection (typically close to 45 degrees in ideal conditions, but can vary depending on release height) is crucial. By optimizing this angle, an athlete can maximize the distance covered and potentially break a record.

* B) Acceleration of the athlete: While the athlete's acceleration during the take-off phase contributes to their initial velocity, which is important for the projection, "acceleration of the athlete" as a general factor is less specific than the angle of projection. The initial velocity (result of acceleration over distance) is important, but without the optimal angle, even a high initial velocity might not yield the best distance.

C) Body shape of the athlete: Body shape can have a minor impact due to air resistance, but its effect is generally negligible compared to the angle of projection and initial velocity for most athletic events. It's not the main factor for breaking a record.

D) Weight of the athlete: In the absence of air resistance, the weight of a projectile does not affect its trajectory or range. While air resistance does exist, weight is not the primary determinant for record-breaking performance in these types of events. The force generated and the angle of projection are far more significant.

questions.text: The given diagram shows a ticker tape. The frequency of the ticker tape is 20 Hz.How much time will be taken by the ticker tape to plot dots from P to T?

options.explanation: * Frequency of the ticker tape (f) = 20 Hz * The diagram shows dots labeled P, Q, R, S, T.Calculations: * Calculate the time period (T) for one interval: The time period is the inverse of the frequency. T = 1 / f = 1 / 20 Hz = 0.05 seconds. This means that 0.05 seconds elapses between the plotting of two consecutive dots. * Count the number of intervals from P to T: Looking at the diagram, the dots are P, Q, R, S, T. * Interval 1: From P to Q * Interval 2: From Q to R * Interval 3: From R to S * Interval 4: From S to T Visually, there are 4 intervals between point P and point T. * Calculate the total time for 4 intervals: Total Time = Number of intervals × Time per interval Total Time = 4 × 0.05 s = 0.20 s.

questions.text: Which of the following pieces of ticker tape shows that the object is speeding up its motion?(Note: Suppose that the left hand side of the ticker tape shows the initial point of the tape.)
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The distance between the dots remains roughly the same throughout the tape. This indicates that the object is covering equal distances in equal time intervals, meaning it is moving at a constant velocity.

On the left (the start), the dots are very close together. As you move to the right, the gaps between the dots get progressively larger. This shows the object is covering more distance in each time interval, which means it is accelerating (speeding up).

On the left, the dots start far apart. As you move toward the right, the gaps become smaller and smaller until the dots are clustered together. This indicates the object is decelerating (slowing down).
This tape is blank. If an object were stationary, the ticker timer would simply keep striking the same spot, resulting in a single heavy ink blot rather than a trail of dots.

questions.explanation: A ticker timer makes dots at regular intervals of time. If the dots are far apart, the object moved a long distance in that time (high speed). If they are close together, it moves a short distance (low speed).Since the note specifies that the left side is the starting point, we read the motion from left to right. According to this, Option B would be correct as the distance between the dots increases from left to right.
